entry.title: Women Tech AUSAPE - Inspirar y colaborar para avanzar
entry.content:
"Inspirar y colaborar para avanzar"

PRIMER EVENTO EN MADRID DE Women Tech AUSAPE

REGISTRO EN LA PAGINA DE AUSAPE 👉 Link de registro.

Agenda: 

16:30h - 17:00h | Recepción de asistentes

17:00h - 17:05h | APERTURA:

  • Sara Antuñano (vicepresidenta AUSAPE). ECOFIN resp. G&l y Proyectos
  • María Rosa Ribó (vocal mujer y tecnología). Directora de proyectos IT, Abacus cooperativa

17:05h - 17:20h | Presentación informe: Women in Business 2024 de la consultora Grant Thornton.

  • Carol Lago - directora del departamento de Consultoría de Negocio e Innovación

17:20h - 17:35h | ENTREVISTA INSPIRADORA: SORPRESA

17:35h - 18:15h | MESA REDONDA con asociaciones:

  • BWN SAP - Susana Muñoz, Team Leader Enterprise Customer Success Manager y Business Woman Network Lead
  • Technovation Girls - Vanesa Cabral, Consultor Sr en Practica Global
  • Women4Cyber - Maica Aguilar, Vocal Junta y Gerente de Cumplimiento y Gestión de Identidades en Ferrovial
  • STEM Talend Girl - Estefanía Serrano, Delegada Territorial de la Fundación ASTI

18:15h - 19:05h | MESA REDONDA con asociados:

  • Sandra Isart, CIO Nexus Energía
  • María José Lodeiros Zubiria: Responsable del equipo SAP BTP en Minsait; premio DonaTic en Cataluña y premio StemWoman
  • Ana Shopovski Mitrovich - Managing Partner y SAP Analyst, ABAP and Integration Consultant en Code10

19:05h - 19:15h | Próximas acciones de la vocalia y cierre:

  • María Rosa Ribó (vocal mujer y tecnología). Directora de proyectos IT, Abacus cooperativa
  • Ana Encinas Directora General de AUSAPE

19:15h - 19:25h | CLAUSURA: Gema Sanz, Directora de Relaciones Institucionales de Madrid Network

19:25h - 20:30h | Cóctel & networking

entry.title: Re: "SAP Integration Suite application is not subscribed"...
entry.content:
A workaround to this is assigning the roles to your user account manually and instantiating an instance of the SAP Process Integration Runtime service.... 

  1. Once the capabilities are active, assign the roles in the screenshot.
  2. Assign the SAP Process Integration Runtime entitlement to the subaccount.
  3. Create a new instance of SAP Process Integration Runtime
  4. Create a service key in the SAP Process Integration Runtime instance. It will provide you the credentials required.

No need to run the booster anymore after completing the steps described above.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Madrid 2025 - Mar 21, 2025
entry.content:
¡Hola Madrid!

We are very excited to announce that we are resuming the SAP Inside Track Madrid in 2025. It will take place next March 21, 2025 @ SAP offices in Madrid, 🇪🇸. The space for the event is limited and we can only host 45 attendees. Make sure to RSVP fast!

An SAP Inside Track (SIT) is a local grassroots community event where SAP Community members come together to share knowledge about SAP related topics. The event is driven/organized by the community, everyone who is interested may participate. Everyone can benefit or contribute: act as presenter, take part in discussions, exchange information, realize networking or enjoy the social get-togethers in the evening.

You can check out all the details about the event in https://sitmadrid.com. Also, if you are interested in speaking 🗣️ at the event? Let us know by submitting your proposal here.

Come to learn and share your knowledge with your peers as well as make new friends in an informal atmosphere. SAP Inside Track events offer SAP Community provided sessions from SAP Experts and passionate SAP Community members.

We will also host an SAP CodeJam the day before (Mar 20, 2025) - 🇪🇸 Service integration with SAP Cloud Application Programming model (Madrid, Spain). You can register for the event here - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/service-integration-with-sap-cloud-application-programming-model-madrid/ec-p/13962160.

The space for the event is limited and we can only host 45 attendees. First come first serve. We will manage a waitlist so even if we've reached 45 RSVPs please still register as some might not be able to make it to the event.

See you in March 2025!

entry.title: Re: Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform
entry.content: @mamartins it is working... I can see many commits in the past hours - https://github.com/ajmaradiaga/feeds/commits/main/scmt . Is there a particular feeds that you are interested in, which you are having issues with?

entry.title: Re: "SAP Integration Suite application is not subscribed"...
entry.content: @Sliao74, yes... the booster is no longer needed if you do the workaround suggested. Can you please accept the solution if it actually help you work around the issue.

entry.title: Re: 🇳🇱 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Zoetermeer, NL)
entry.content:
@AxelMinnemann is correct @mahi_angam20 … just RSVPing is enough. We keep track of the order that people have sign up in case the maximum capacity is reached. A few weeks before the event we send confirmation emails/reminders.

Looking forward to meeting you both at the event.

entry.title: Re: Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform
entry.content: @mamartins hmmm can you share which specific feed have you imported in Outlook? It is just to check on my end and test it myself.

entry.title: Re: Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform
entry.content: @mamartins Thanks for sharing... I can see that it is possible to access the URL if you open it in a browser but I'm experiencing a similar behaviour when trying to get the out of the box RSS from a reader.

entry.title: 🇪🇸 Service integration with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam introduces attendees to service integration with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). Specifically, the Node.js flavor of CAP is used.

During this event, you'll become acquainted with how external services can be integrated and consumed from within a CAP project. In working through the exercises you'll also become comfortable with various facilities that CDS offers for declaring, relating and exposing entities, and familiar with how the SAP Cloud SDK covers connectivity and destinations, supporting such integration scenarios with CAP.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday 20 Mar 2025 in Madrid, Spain. It is part of the SIT Madrid 2025 event that is running the day after. Both events will be at the SAP office in Madrid.

You can find more details about the SAP Inside Track Madrid 2025 and registration details here - https://community.sap.com/t5/madrid-events/sap-inside-track-madrid-2025-mar-21-2025/ev-p/13944045

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-service-integration-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page. Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees for this event. In that context, if you indicate that you're attending and later realise you can't, please remove your name so that others get a chance instead. Thanks!

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform
entry.content: @rkaouass , I'm not entirely sure what's going on. It does work with some RSS readers but others are being blocked.

entry.title: Re: Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform
entry.content: @mamartins @rkaouass I was facing the same issue when trying to refresh the feed when using Miniflux. I was able to work around it by specifying a custom user agent in the feed configuration, e.g. Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:93.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/93.0. I'm not sure this is possible in the readers that you are using.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite – Connect Everything
entry.content: Looking forward to the video series 😉

entry.title: Re: 🔴 Filtering of RAP business events in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and in AEM
entry.content:
Filtering is supported in S4HC you can see it in the video or here as well - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q4H0LNZi7Dg&list=PL6RpkC85SLQDTp97PAUVi6KYKRHIKtaii&index=2. That said, I believe you are referring to filtering in the event broker, which is handled differently in EMIS/AEM compared to Event Hub.

  • In EMIS/AEM, all the filtering that can be done is via topics, aka topic-based filtering. Therefore, it is important to surface the data at the topic level if you want to do some filtering in EMIS/AEM.
  • In Event Hub this is a bit different because it allows you to do content-based filtering. In this case, you can configure filters based on the data that's available in the CloudEvent header fields.

entry.title: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩
entry.content:
The second SAP Developer Challenge of the year is now available! The Developer Advocates have created the SAP Community Escape House - a virtual escape house (consisting of multiple rooms) that you can play in your browser. Read on for all the details.

SAP Developer Challenges are all about having fun, trying new things, and learning along the way. You can also earn a badge in the SAP Community upon successful completion. For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges will happen in the SAP Community Escape House. For each challenge, we will unlock a new room in the house, and they all have a specific focus topic. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding-related tasks/questions. This month is all about Integration, specifically the optional hybrid integration runtime offered as part of SAP Integration Suite.

The details of January's challenge (UI5) are here: link.

How to participate

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID. If you don't know your user ID, go to your SAP Community profile. You can find the numeric ID at the end of the URL.

SAP Community Escape House - Enter your SAP Community ID


Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the Integration room and start exploring it.

SAP Community Escape House - Available rooms

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips. Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and, eventually, instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room. The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

SAP Community Escape House - Integration room

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

The schedule

The integration room was unlocked on February 3rd and will stay unlocked until the end of the year. However, the challenge closes on February 28th, so you will have to escape the room until then to qualify for a badge. Unlike the previous year, this SAP Developer Challenge does not consist of weekly sub-challenges. The next room (next challenge) will be unlocked in March.

Good luck and happy escaping!

entry.title: Re: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩
entry.content: @DG Thanks for letting me know. We will fix it 🙂

entry.title: Re: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩
entry.content: @Juan-Cárdenas @Ihor_Haranichev you are right... I just checked the question and there is something odd. Let me get back to you on this.

entry.title: Re: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩
entry.content: @Juan-Cárdenas @Ihor_Haranichev , can you please check again. @nicoschoenteich just pushed the fix :-). Sorry that I messed up the question hehe

entry.title: 🇦🇪 SAP CodeJam: Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite (Dubai, UAE)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam instance will be about SAP Integration Suite and how we can use it to connect systems and services in our landscape. Join us for this 5-hour event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Dubai, UAE.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one below in the diagram by the end of the CodeJam.

 

If you are interested in what we will develop during the event, check out the Connecting Systems and Services using SAP Integration Suite CodeJam GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/connecting-systems-services-integration-suite-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will use during the event.

This is a free hands-on, in-person event only (not virtual) and takes place Tuesday, the 25th of March, in Dubai, UAE @ the Emirates Financial Towers (2308 23rd floor, South Tower, Emirates Financial Tower, DIFC). The event is kindly being hosted by Mark9 solutions, thank you! 

The instructor is Developer Advocate Antonio Maradiaga.

Every attendee must complete essential prerequisites before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/connecting-systems-services-integration-suite-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page. Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees for this event. In that context, if you indicate that you're attending and later realise you can't, please remove your name so that others get a chance instead. Thanks!

Location: 2308 23rd floor, South Tower, Emirates Financial Tower, DIFC

entry.title: Re: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩
entry.content: @mwnthanks for the feedback.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite – Connect Everything
entry.content: Tomorrow we will be premiering the first video of a new SAP Tech Bytes video series - Connect everything with SAP Integration Suite. In this first instance, Desirée Degutis will show us how we can leverage pre-built content to enable the integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and Salesforce. Make sure to tune in on Wednesday 12th of Feb at 10:00 CET - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HWPRztVhtzE
